---
title: "Alex Zhong"
image: files/profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 16em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:a4zhong@ucsd.edu
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/guanzong-zhong-58a4643a8/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/rsm-a4zhong
---

I'm a Master's student in **Business Analytics** at UC San Diego's Rady School of Management, where I focus on experimentation, marketing analytics, and applied causal inference.

Before Rady, I worked in investment management at two firms in Guangzhou and led a team during Citibank's summer analyst program. I hold a B.Sc. in Mathematics, Operational Research, Statistics and Economics (MORSE) from the University of Warwick.

I'm interested in how data-driven decisions get made — and, more often, how they fail to. Most of what I write here is an attempt to think clearly about that, with code.

## Background

**Education**
University of California, San Diego — MS Business Analytics (2025–2026)
University of Warwick — B.Sc. MORSE

**Languages**
Mandarin (native) · Cantonese (fluent) · English (fluent, GRE 331)

**Currently**
Working on a supply chain & customer loyalty analytics capstone with Thermo Fisher Scientific.
